proof_trace: damon_free_target() (core.c:335-342) frees regions and kfrees the target struct but never calls put_pid(t->pid); the sysfs layer's damon_sysfs_before_terminate callback (sysfs.c:2308), which correctly calls put_pid(), is registered only after damon_sysfs_apply_inputs() succeeds (sysfs.c:2449). When damon_sysfs_set_targets() succeeds (vaddr targets added via find_get_pid()) but damon_sysfs_set_schemes() returns -ENOMEM, damon_sysfs_build_ctx() calls damon_destroy_ctx() → damon_destroy_targets() which leaks every struct pid reference held by those targets.
attack_scenario: A privileged user configures DAMON via sysfs with one or more vaddr/fvaddr monitoring targets (valid PIDs) plus enough schemes to exhaust memory during damon_sysfs_mk_scheme(); the resulting ENOMEM error triggers damon_destroy_ctx() on the half-built context, leaking one struct pid reference per added target — repeated invocations can progressively exhaust struct pid objects or prevent PID reuse.
reason: damon_free_target()がput_pid(t->pid)を呼ばず、pidリファレンスリーク。kdamondシャットダウンパスで発生。最新ではcleanup_targetコールバック追加で修正済み。GT外の新発見。

proof_trace: In damon_sysfs_add_target(), t is allocated via damon_new_target() (kmalloc) at line 2180. When damon_target_has_pid(ctx) is true and find_get_pid() returns NULL (lines 2185-2188), the code jumps to destroy_targets_out before damon_add_target(ctx, t) at line 2190 — so t is never inserted into ctx->adaptive_targets. The cleanup call damon_sysfs_destroy_targets(ctx) at line 2197 only iterates targets already in the context list, so t is never freed.
attack_scenario: A local user with sysfs write access writes a nonexistent PID to a VADDR monitoring context target (e.g., /sys/kernel/mm/damon/admin/kdamonds/0/contexts/0/targets/0/pid_target), causing find_get_pid() to return NULL and leaking sizeof(struct damon_target) bytes of kernel memory per write; repeated triggering can exhaust kernel memory.
reason: damon_sysfs_add_target()でdamon_new_target()確保のtがfind_get_pid()失敗時にctxリストに未挿入のまま解放されない。damon_sysfs_destroy_targets()はリスト内のみ解放するためtがリーク。proof_traceの「sysfs kobjectリーク」記述は不正確だがメモリリーク自体は実在。コード実確認済み。最新では修正済み（damon_add_targetをfind_get_pid前に移動し、呼び出し側のクリーンアップでカバー）。

proof_trace: ctx allocated by damon_new_ctx() at L434 is not freed when damon_select_ops() fails at L438-439: the function returns -EINVAL directly without calling damon_destroy_ctx(ctx). The later damon_new_target() failure at L446 correctly calls damon_destroy_ctx(ctx), confirming developer awareness of the cleanup pattern, but the earlier path is missing it. No module_exit or deferred cleanup exists to recover the allocation.
attack_scenario: If DAMON_OPS_PADDR is not registered at module load time (e.g., dependency ordering issue or kernel built without paddr ops support), damon_select_ops() returns -EINVAL and the struct damon_ctx allocation from damon_new_ctx() is permanently leaked. This is a one-time kernel memory leak at module initialization.
